concerns.text: FR-011 mandates a 'human-verified subset' to validate label noise. Task T036 implements a 'random label flip noise model' (synthetic simulation) instead of a task for actual human review. This silently relaxes the constitutional requirement for human verification, replacing empirical validation with a simulation.

FR-006 and SC-002 explicitly require a 'multiple linear regression' model and 'adjusted R²'. Task T030 implements 'Logistic Regression' and 'Pseudo-R²' based on a plan override. While the plan notes this as a methodological fix, the task list does not include a corresponding task to formally update the spec/SC or document the change as a sanctioned deviation, creating a semantic gap where the deliverable (Logistic Regression) does not match the spec requirement (Linear Regression) without explicit traceability.

FR-004 and US-2 explicitly require 'embedding-based similarity to known vulnerable patterns (using a pre-trained code encoder)'. Tasks T018-T020 cover structural and semantic metrics but lack a specific task to load a pre-trained code encoder and compute the embedding similarity vector. The deliverable for FR-004 is incomplete.

Task T013 hardcodes the model to 'microsoft/Phi-mini-4k-instruct'. The spec (Assumptions/FR-002) lists multiple candidate models (CodeLlamaB, StarCoder-Base, distilled Llama-2) without specifying a single default. This task silently narrows the experimental scope to one model, violating the requirement to evaluate the effectiveness of LLMs (plural) or at least the flexibility to choose from the specified set.
